ashwagandha.name: Ashwagandha
ashwagandha.traditional_uses: Rasayana (rejuvenation), Vajikarana (vitality), balances Vata and Kapha doshas. Used for weakness, fatigue, nervous system disorders, and reproductive health.
ashwagandha.modern_research: Classified as an adaptogen; reduces cortisol levels by 27-30% (clinical studies). Shows anxiolytic properties comparable to lorazepam in animal studies. Anti-inflammatory via withanolides. Improves VO2 max and endurance.
ashwagandha.how_to_use: 300-600mg standardized extract daily, OR 1 tsp root powder in warm milk at bedtime. Can be taken with ghee and honey.
ashwagandha.contraindications: Pregnancy, autoimmune conditions (MS, lupus, rheumatoid arthritis), thyroid disorders (without supervision), sedative medications.

triphala.name: Triphala
triphala.traditional_uses: Tridosha balancing formula (Amalaki-Pitta, Bibhitaki-Kapha, Haritaki-Vata). Digestive tonic, mild laxative, eye health, diabetes management, Rasayana.
triphala.modern_research: Prebiotic properties supporting gut microbiome. Strong antioxidant activity (ORAC score). Anti-diabetic properties via alpha-glucosidase inhibition. Antimicrobial against H. pylori.
triphala.how_to_use: 1 tsp powder in warm water before bed, OR 500mg capsule twice daily. For eye wash: dilute in boiled cooled water.
triphala.contraindications: Pregnancy, diarrhea or loose stools, before surgery (blood thinning effect), iron deficiency (take separately from iron supplements).

brahmi.name: Brahmi
brahmi.reference: Charaka Samhita, Kalpasthana — Medhya Rasayana group
brahmi.traditional_uses: Medhya Rasayana (nootropic). Enhances memory, intelligence, speech. Balances all doshas esp. Vata and Pitta. Treats epilepsy, anxiety, skin diseases.
brahmi.modern_research: Bacosides A and B promote neuronal synthesis. Meta-analyses show significant improvement in attention and memory consolidation. Adaptogen properties. Reduces anxiety via GABA modulation.
brahmi.how_to_use: 300-450mg standardized extract daily with meals. OR fresh juice 5-10ml. Brahmi ghee (medicated) for topical application on scalp.
brahmi.contraindications: May slow heart rate — avoid with bradycardia medications. Consult before use in hypothyroidism. High doses may cause nausea.

tulsi.name: Tulsi
tulsi.traditional_uses: Kapha and Vata reducing. Used for respiratory disorders, fever, cough, asthma. Antimicrobial, digestive stimulant, adaptogen. Considered sacred in Ayurveda.
tulsi.modern_research: Eugenol shows COX-2 inhibition comparable to NSAIDs. Significant immunomodulatory activity. Anti-stress adaptogen comparable to Panax ginseng. Antibacterial against E. coli, S. aureus.
tulsi.how_to_use: 10-20 fresh leaves daily as tea or chewed raw. OR 300-600mg dried extract. Tulsi tea: 5-7 fresh leaves in boiling water, steep 5 minutes.
tulsi.contraindications: May interfere with blood clotting — avoid 2 weeks before surgery. Use carefully during pregnancy (uterine stimulant). May lower blood sugar.

neem.name: Neem
neem.traditional_uses: Pitta and Kapha reducing. Anti-parasitic, anti-fungal, skin diseases (Kushta), fever (Jwara), diabetes (Prameha), blood purification (Rakta Shodhana).
neem.modern_research: Azadirachtin and nimbin show potent antimalarial, antifungal, and insecticidal properties. Anti-inflammatory via prostaglandin inhibition. Anti-hyperglycemic effects in clinical trials. Antibiofilm activity against oral bacteria.
neem.how_to_use: Neem leaves: 1-2g powder with water. Neem oil topically for skin conditions. Twig as natural toothbrush (chew stick). Capsules 500mg twice daily for blood sugar support.
neem.contraindications: Pregnancy (abortifacient), infants (toxic), organ transplant patients (immunosuppressive). Avoid with immunosuppressant drugs. Not for long-term internal use without supervision.

shatavari.name: Shatavari
shatavari.traditional_uses: Primary female tonic — balances hormones, supports lactation, menstrual health. Pitta and Vata reducing. Immunity enhancer, anti-inflammatory, gastroprotective. Considered the queen of herbs for women.
shatavari.modern_research: Steroidal saponins (shatavarins) show estrogen-like activity. Significant galactagogue in clinical trials. Immunomodulatory via macrophage activation. Gastroprotective via mucin secretion. Anti-oxidant and anti-stress.
shatavari.how_to_use: 1-2 tsp root powder in warm milk daily, OR 500-1000mg extract. Shatavari ghee for women's health. Best taken with milk and ghee for absorption.
shatavari.contraindications: Estrogen-sensitive conditions (breast cancer, endometriosis) — consult doctor. Excess water retention in high doses. Asparagus allergy.

giloy.name: Giloy (Guduchi)
giloy.traditional_uses: Tridosha balancing. Immune modulator, anti-pyretic (fever), anti-rheumatic, liver tonic. Called 'Amrita' (divine nectar) due to remarkable health properties. Anti-cancer and anti-diabetic.
giloy.modern_research: Tinosporine and berberine show immunomodulatory effects — increases macrophage activity. Significant anti-malarial properties. Anti-diabetic via alpha-glucosidase inhibition. Hepatoprotective against CCl4 toxicity. Anti-arthritic comparable to prednisolone.
giloy.how_to_use: Fresh stem juice 10-20ml daily, OR 1g powder twice daily. Giloy satva (starch extract) 250-500mg with honey. Decoction: boil 1-inch stem in 2 cups water until reduced to 1 cup.
giloy.contraindications: Autoimmune conditions (stimulates immune system). May lower blood sugar — monitor with diabetes meds. Avoid in pregnancy. Can cause constipation in high doses.

amla.name: Amla (Amalaki)
amla.traditional_uses: Greatest Rasayana herb. Pitta reducing and all-dosha balancer. Eye health, digestion, liver support, anti-aging, immunity, hair growth. Key ingredient in Triphala and Chyavanprash.
amla.modern_research: Highest natural Vitamin C content (20x oranges, heat-stable due to tannin binding). Strong antioxidant ORAC score. Anti-atherogenic — reduces LDL oxidation. Anti-diabetic via alpha-glucosidase inhibition. Significant hair growth promotion (comparable to minoxidil in some studies).
amla.how_to_use: 1-2 fresh fruits daily, OR 1tsp powder in water. Amla juice 10-20ml morning on empty stomach. Amla candy (murabba) for consistent use.
amla.contraindications: High doses may worsen dry skin/hair in Vata types. Mild blood thinning — caution before surgery. May reduce iron absorption (take separately from iron).

haritaki.name: Haritaki
haritaki.reference: Charaka Samhita, Sutrasthana Ch. 4 (called 'mother of herbs')
haritaki.traditional_uses: Primarily Vata reducing. Called 'mother of herbs' in Ayurveda. Digestive tonic, mild laxative, anti-aging, eye health, wound healing, respiratory support. One of the three fruits in Triphala.
haritaki.modern_research: Chebulinic acid and ellagic acid show potent antioxidant activity. Antibacterial against multi-drug resistant bacteria. Anti-HIV activity in vitro. Anti-cancer properties via apoptosis induction. Significant anti-diabetic effect.
haritaki.how_to_use: 500mg powder with warm water at bedtime for constipation. Chew small piece after meals for digestion. Decoction for gargle in throat infections. Different preparations for different seasons.
haritaki.contraindications: Pregnancy, severe dehydration, high fevers. Avoid in wasting diseases (depleting action). High doses may cause loose stools.

bibhitaki.name: Bibhitaki
bibhitaki.traditional_uses: Primarily Kapha reducing. Respiratory disorders, cough, hoarseness, bronchitis. Eye and hair health. One of the three fruits of Triphala — the Kapha-balancing component.
bibhitaki.modern_research: Gallic acid shows potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity. Antibacterial against respiratory pathogens. Anti-mutagenic properties. Lipid-lowering effects in animal studies. Anti-platelet aggregation.
bibhitaki.how_to_use: 500mg powder with honey for respiratory issues. As part of Triphala 1 tsp in warm water. Decoction: 3-5g in 200ml water reduced to 50ml for respiratory complaints.
bibhitaki.contraindications: Avoid in severe constipation or dry bowel conditions. Excess use may cause dryness. Caution in Vata constitutions without proper anupana (vehicle).

turmeric.name: Turmeric (Haridra)
turmeric.traditional_uses: Tridosha balancer, especially Kapha and Pitta. Wound healing, anti-inflammatory, skin disorders (Kushta), diabetes, liver disorders, respiratory issues. Used in Panchakarma and daily cooking.
turmeric.modern_research: Curcumin shows NF-kB inhibition — comparable to cortisone without side effects. 6,000+ studies in PubMed. Anti-cancer, neuroprotective, cardioprotective. Bioavailability enhanced 2000% with piperine (black pepper). WHO approved dosage: 1.4mg/kg body weight.
turmeric.how_to_use: Golden milk: 1 tsp in warm milk with black pepper and ghee. 500-1000mg curcumin extract with piperine. Fresh rhizome 1-3g daily. Topically as paste for wounds.
turmeric.contraindications: Gallstones or bile duct obstruction. Blood thinning medications (high doses). May worsen acid reflux in sensitive individuals. Avoid high doses in pregnancy.

ginger.name: Ginger (Shunti)
ginger.reference: Charaka Samhita, Sutrasthana Ch. 27 (Mahabheshaja — great medicine)
ginger.traditional_uses: Called 'Mahabheshaja' — the universal medicine. Vata and Kapha reducing. Digestive fire (Agni) stimulant, anti-nausea, carminative, anti-inflammatory. Essential in Trikatu formula.
ginger.modern_research: Gingerols and shogaols inhibit COX and LOX pathways — anti-inflammatory. Meta-analyses confirm efficacy for nausea (pregnancy, chemo). Anti-diabetic via AMPK activation. GERD relief. Osteoarthritis pain reduction comparable to NSAIDs.
ginger.how_to_use: Fresh ginger tea: 1-inch slice in hot water. 500-1000mg dry ginger extract. Trikatu (ginger+pepper+pippali) churna 500mg before meals. Ginger-honey-lemon in warm water for immunity.
ginger.contraindications: High doses with blood thinners (warfarin). Gallstones — moderate amounts only. Third trimester pregnancy — consult doctor. Acid reflux may worsen with excessive use.

cumin.name: Cumin (Jiraka)
cumin.traditional_uses: Vata and Kapha reducing. Digestive stimulant, carminative, anti-colic. Post-partum recovery, milk production stimulation, fever reduction. Used in cumin water (Jira kashaya) daily.
cumin.modern_research: Cuminaldehyde shows significant antimicrobial activity. Improves gut motility and reduces IBS symptoms in clinical trials. Anti-diabetic via improved insulin sensitivity. Iron content supports anemia management. Anti-oxidant DPPH activity.
cumin.how_to_use: Cumin water: boil 1 tsp in 2 cups water until reduced to 1 cup. Jeera rice and digestive recipes. Roasted cumin powder 500mg after meals. 3g daily for digestive issues.
cumin.contraindications: Hypoglycemia (blood sugar lowering effect). Excessive intake may cause liver toxicity. Blood thinning effect — caution pre-surgery. Avoid excess in acid peptic disorders.

licorice.name: Licorice (Yashtimadhu)
licorice.reference: Charaka Samhita, Sutrasthana Ch. 4 (Jivaniya group — life-promoting)
licorice.traditional_uses: Pitta and Vata reducing. Medhya (brain tonic), anti-ulcer, cough, sore throat, eye health, skin lightening. Harmonizer in herbal formulas. Key ingredient in many Rasayana preparations.
licorice.modern_research: Glycyrrhizin shows anti-viral activity against herpes, hepatitis C, HIV. Significant anti-ulcer efficacy (comparable to H2 blockers). Anti-inflammatory via cortisol metabolism. Adaptogen and anti-stress. Anti-cancer via GL inhibition of NF-kB.
licorice.how_to_use: Licorice tea: 1-3g root in hot water. Mulethi powder 1-3g with honey for cough. Yashtimadhu ghrita for eye and brain health. Not more than 4-6 weeks continuously.
licorice.contraindications: Hypertension (glycyrrhizin raises blood pressure). Hypokalemia risk. Pregnancy (uterine stimulant). Kidney or liver disease. Avoid with corticosteroids. Maximum 4 weeks continuous use.

shankhapushpi.name: Shankhapushpi
shankhapushpi.reference: Charaka Samhita, Kalpasthana (Medhya Rasayana — intelligence enhancer)
shankhapushpi.traditional_uses: Primary Medhya Rasayana — enhances intellect, memory, concentration. Pitta reducing, anti-epileptic, anti-anxiety, thyroid regulation, sleep improvement.
shankhapushpi.modern_research: Ethanolic extract shows significant memory enhancement in animal models. Reduces anxiety via GABA-A receptor modulation. Thyroid-reducing activity — reduces T4 levels. Antiepileptic effect comparable to phenobarbital. Neuroprotective against aluminum-induced neurotoxicity.
shankhapushpi.how_to_use: 3-6g powder with milk morning and evening. OR 10-20ml fresh juice. Shankhapushpi syrup (classical preparation) 10ml twice daily. Best taken with milk for brain benefits.
shankhapushpi.contraindications: May interact with phenytoin (reduces absorption). Hypothyroidism — lowers thyroid hormones. Caution in pregnancy. Not for long-term use without monitoring.

vidari.name: Vidari
vidari.traditional_uses: Vata and Pitta balancing. Rejuvenative tonic (Rasayana), reproductive health, lactation support, muscle building, anti-aging. Particularly useful in convalescence and debility.
vidari.modern_research: Puerarin and isoflavones show significant estrogenic activity. Cardioprotective — reduces cardiac fibrosis. Neuroprotective via antioxidant activity. Hepatoprotective. Anti-diabetic via GLUT-4 upregulation.
vidari.how_to_use: 3-6g powder with milk and honey. OR decoction 40-80ml twice daily. Vidari kanda avaleha (jam) for sustained release. Best taken with milk for anabolic effects.
vidari.contraindications: Hormone-sensitive conditions (similar to phytoestrogens). Obesity — high caloric density. Avoid in excess Kapha. Consult doctor with HRT or OCP use.

punarnava.name: Punarnava
punarnava.traditional_uses: Tridosha balancing, especially Kapha. Meaning 'that which renews' — rejuvenates the body. Primary herb for edema (Shotha), kidney disorders (Mutrakrichra), liver diseases, anemia, heart conditions.
punarnava.modern_research: Punarnavine shows significant diuretic activity (comparable to furosemide). Anti-inflammatory via COX-2 inhibition. Hepatoprotective against liver toxicity. Anti-fibrotic in kidney disease models. Iron-rich — supports anemia treatment. Anti-cancer via apoptosis.
punarnava.how_to_use: 3-6g root powder with water twice daily. Punarnava mandura (classical iron preparation) for anemia. Fresh leaf juice 10-20ml for kidney support. Punarnava asava (fermented preparation) for chronic conditions.
punarnava.contraindications: Diuretic effect — caution with blood pressure medications. Avoid in dehydration. May enhance effects of diuretic drugs. Not recommended in severe kidney failure.
